factors.summary: Overall demand is stable with expected growth in specific segments.

evidence.text: We expect our consolidated sales to grow between ten percent and 10.75 percent in 2018. In our North America segment, we project our sales in the U.S. will grow in 2018 compared to 2017 due to higher residential water heater and boiler volumes resulting from expected industry-wide new construction growth and expansion of replacement demand.

factors.summary: Order backlog is stable with new orders outpacing revenue growth.

evidence.text: Our sales in the first quarter of 2018 were $788 million, approximately 6.5 percent higher than sales of $740 million in the first quarter of 2017. Higher sales in the first quarter of 2018 compared to the first quarter of 2017 were primarily due to continued demand for our consumer products in China and positive end markets for our boilers in North America.
evidence.subsection: FIRST THREE MONTHS OF 2018 COMPARED TO 2017

factors.summary: The company successfully raised prices, with a 10% average increase effective in early June 2018.

evidence.text: In the first quarter of 2018, we announced a price increase, which averaged ten percent, on our U.S. wholesale water heaters due to steel, freight and other cost inflation. The price increase will be effective in early June.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin products, particularly in North America with water treatment products.

evidence.text: We continued to expand our North America water treatment product offerings with the acquisition of Hague Quality Water International (Hague) on September 5, 2017. We expect sales of our North America water treatment products to increase by more than 75 percent in 2018, compared to 2017.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is expected to accelerate, supported by stable water heater replacement demand and new product introductions.

evidence.text: Combining all of these factors, we expect our consolidated sales to grow between ten percent and 10.75 percent in 2018. With solid performance in the first quarter and our expected organic growth, supported by stable water heater replacement demand, we have increased the midpoint of our EPS and adjusted EPS guidance for 2018.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, particularly in North America's residential and commercial water heater markets.

evidence.text: We expect the North America residential water heater industry to have three to 3.5 percent unit growth in 2018. In addition, we expect our sales in India to grow over 40 percent in 2018 from approximately $26 million in 2017.

factors.summary: The fastest growing end markets are in North America's residential and commercial sectors and China's tankless water heater market.

evidence.text: In our North America segment, we project our sales in the U.S. will grow in 2018 compared to 2017 due to higher residential water heater and boiler volumes... In our Rest of World segment, we expect sales in China to grow in 2018 at a rate of approximately nine to ten percent in local currency, or over 14 percent in U.S. dollar terms, as we believe tankless water heater market growth...

factors.summary: Raw material costs are rising, particularly steel and freight costs, which are being partially offset by price increases.

evidence.text: The price increase will be effective in early June. Our sales of boilers grew 13 percent in 2017, and we expect ten percent sales growth in 2018, driven by the continuing U.S. industry transition to higher efficiency products and our introduction of new products.

factors.summary: Gross profit margin is stable, while operating margins are under pressure due to restructuring costs and increased expenses.

evidence.text: First quarter gross profit margin of 40.8 percent in 2018 was essentially equal to gross profit margin of 40.7 percent in the first quarter of 2017. Selling, general and administrative (SG&A) expenses for the first quarter of 2018 were $192.9 million or $9.7 million higher than SG&A expenses of $183.2 million in the first quarter of 2017.
evidence.subsection: FIRST THREE MONTHS OF 2018 COMPARED TO 2017

factors.summary: Restructuring actions are ongoing, with $6.7 million in pre-tax restructuring expenses in the first quarter of 2018.

evidence.text: As a result of the relocation of production, we incurred pre-tax restructuring and impairment expenses of $6.7 million in the first quarter of 2018, primarily related to employee severance and compensation related costs, building lease exit costs, and the impairment of assets.
evidence.subsection: FIRST THREE MONTHS OF 2018 COMPARED TO 2017

factors.summary: Productivity initiatives are ongoing, with restructuring and capacity utilization improvements expected to contribute to margin expansion.

evidence.text: We expect full year adjusted segment margin to be between 21.75 and 22.25 percent in 2018, negatively impacted by start-up and transition costs related to our recently announced new water treatment product business at Lowe’s home improvement stores in the U.S.
evidence.subsection: FIRST THREE MONTHS OF 2018 COMPARED TO 2017

factors.summary: Supply chain health is stable with no significant disruptions mentioned.

evidence.text: Currency translation added approximately $3 million to segment earnings in the first quarter of 2018 compared to the first quarter of 2017. The first quarter segment margin of 12.3 percent in 2018 was modestly lower than our segment margin of 12.5 percent in the same period last year.
evidence.subsection: FIRST THREE MONTHS OF 2018 COMPARED TO 2017

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is expected to increase with planned production transfers and new business expansions.

evidence.text: In March 2018, we announced a plan to transfer water heater, boiler, and storage tank production from our Renton, Washington plant to our other U.S. plants. The majority of the consolidation of operations will occur in the second quarter of 2018.
evidence.subsection: FIRST THREE MONTHS OF 2018 COMPARED TO 2017

factors.summary: Inventory levels are normalizing, with no significant risk of obsolescence or shortage mentioned.

evidence.text: Our effective income tax rate for the first quarter of 2018 was 21.2 percent compared to 27.2 percent in the same period last year. Our effective income tax rate in the first quarter of 2018 was lower than the effective income tax rate in the first quarter of 2017 primarily due to lower federal income taxes related to the U.S. Tax Cuts & Jobs Act (U.S. Tax Reform) which were partially offset by lower income tax benefits from settled stock-based compensation awards.
evidence.subsection: FIRST THREE MONTHS OF 2018 COMPARED TO 2017

factors.summary: Product quality and safety incidents are not mentioned as a significant issue.

evidence.text: We are providing non-GAAP measures (adjusted earnings, adjusted earnings per share, and adjusted segment earnings) that exclude restructuring and impairment expenses. Reconciliations to measures on a GAAP basis are provided later in this section.

factors.summary: Management has increased forward guidance for revenue and EPS, reflecting confidence in organic growth.

evidence.text: We believe we will achieve full-year net earnings of between $2.52 and $2.58 per share, which excludes the potential impact from future acquisitions. We believe we will achieve full-year adjusted earnings of between $2.55 and $2.61 per share, which excludes restructuring and impairment expenses and the potential impact from future acquisitions.

factors.summary: Management is confident, as indicated by increased buyback activity and dividend payments.

evidence.text: Depending on factors such as stock price, working capital requirements and alternative investment opportunities, we expect to spend approximately $135 million on stock repurchases through our Rule 10b5-1 automatic trading plan in 2018. In addition, we may opportunistically buy shares in the open market. On April 9, 2018, our Board of Directors declared a cash dividend of $0.18 per share on our Common Stock and Class A common stock.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include geopolitical factors and tariffs, which could impact the supply chain and costs.

evidence.text: Are tariffs, trade restrictions, or export controls positively or negatively affecting the company's sales, costs, or supply chain?
evidence.subsection: Recent Accounting Pronouncements / Forward Looking Statements

factors.summary: Long-term strategic direction is clear, focusing on expanding product lines and entering new markets.

evidence.text: We continued to expand our North America water treatment product offerings with the acquisition of Hague Quality Water International (Hague) on September 5, 2017. We expect sales of our North America water treatment products to increase by more than 75 percent in 2018, compared to 2017.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include foreign exchange movements and interest rates, which are expected to have a moderate impact on results.

evidence.text: Our effective income tax rate for the first quarter of 2018 was 21.2 percent compared to 27.2 percent in the same period last year. Our effective income tax rate in the first quarter of 2018 was lower than the effective income tax rate in the first quarter of 2017 primarily due to lower federal income taxes related to the U.S. Tax Cuts & Jobs Act (U.S. Tax Reform) which were partially offset by lower income tax benefits from settled stock-based compensation awards.
evidence.subsection: FIRST THREE MONTHS OF 2018 COMPARED TO 2017

factors.summary: New product launches and increased business with Lowe’s are key catalysts for growth.

evidence.text: We continued to expand our North America water treatment product offerings with the acquisition of Hague Quality Water International (Hague) on September 5, 2017. We expect sales of our North America water treatment products to increase by more than 75 percent in 2018, compared to 2017, primarily due to our recently announced new business at Lowe’s, a full year of Hague sales and organic volume growth.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are favorable, with growth in key markets such as North America and China supporting demand.

evidence.text: We expect our consolidated sales to grow between ten percent and 10.75 percent in 2018. With solid performance in the first quarter and our expected organic growth, supported by stable water heater replacement demand, we have increased the midpoint of our EPS and adjusted EPS guidance for 2018.

factors.summary: Interest rate changes have had a favorable impact through lower borrowing costs and improved cash flow management.

evidence.text: Our total debt decreased $117.2 million from $410.4 million at December 31, 2017 to $293.2 million at March 31, 2018. Cash repatriation to the U.S. of approximately $212 million was primarily used to pay down floating rate debt, as well as, fund our share repurchase activity and dividend payments. Our leverage, as measured by the ratio of total debt to total capitalization, was 14.6 percent at the end of the first quarter in 2018, compared with 20 percent at the end of last year.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements have been favorable, particularly in China, contributing to higher sales.

evidence.text: Sales in China also benefitted from currency translation of approximately $21 million in the first quarter of 2018 due to the appreciation of the Chinese currency.
evidence.subsection: FIRST THREE MONTHS OF 2018 COMPARED TO 2017

factors.summary: Geopolitical risks are mentioned as potential future headwinds, particularly trade tensions.

evidence.text: Are geopolitical factors (conflicts, sanctions, trade tensions, political instability) creating material risks, disruptions, or opportunities for the company?
evidence.subsection: Recent Accounting Pronouncements / Forward Looking Statements

factors.summary: CapEx is expected to remain stable, with plans to spend approximately $100 million in 2018.

evidence.text: Capital expenditures totaled $17.3 million in the first quarter of 2018, compared with $16.8 million in the year ago period. We project 2018 capital expenditures will be approximately $100 million.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is improving, with a decrease in total debt and lower leverage ratio.

evidence.text: Our total debt decreased $117.2 million from $410.4 million at December 31, 2017 to $293.2 million at March 31, 2018. Our leverage, as measured by the ratio of total debt to total capitalization, was 14.6 percent at the end of the first quarter in 2018, compared with 20 percent at the end of last year.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are being prioritized with increased buyback activity and dividend payments.

evidence.text: Depending on factors such as stock price, working capital requirements and alternative investment opportunities, we expect to spend approximately $135 million on stock repurchases through our Rule 10b5-1 automatic trading plan in 2018. In addition, we may opportunistically buy shares in the open market. On April 9, 2018, our Board of Directors declared a cash dividend of $0.18 per share on our Common Stock and Class A common stock.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving, with expectations of $475 million for the full year 2018.

evidence.text: For the full year 2018, we expect cash provided by operating activities will be approximately $475 million.

factors.summary: Acquisitions are creating value through expansion into new product lines and markets, such as the acquisition of Hague.

evidence.text: We continued to expand our North America water treatment product offerings with the acquisition of Hague Quality Water International (Hague) on September 5, 2017. We expect sales of our North America water treatment products to increase by more than 75 percent in 2018, compared to 2017.

factors.summary: Headcount restructuring is ongoing with layoffs and plant closures to reduce costs.

evidence.text: As a result of the relocation of production, we incurred pre-tax restructuring and impairment expenses of $6.7 million in the first quarter of 2018, primarily related to employee severance and compensation related costs, building lease exit costs and the impairment of assets.

We consider current market conditions including changes in interest rates in making these assumptions.
